## 2024-05-19 - [Fix Information Leakage via RPC Errors]
**Vulnerability:** The application was replying directly to Discord users with the exact RPC error messages generated by the `bitcoin` client daemon. This exposed internal infrastructure details and potential stack traces to unprivileged users, enabling attackers to gain intel.
**Learning:** Raw error messages from underlying dependencies should never be forwarded to users without sanitation or masking. Error handlers need to output generic user-friendly messages while logging the actual details to an internal log stream.
**Prevention:** Always mask `err.message` in replies or API responses and write the raw error only to stdout/stderr or internal loggers.
